name: Empañadas
recipeIngredient: Jam, preserves, or chopped fresh fruit mixed with sugar, raisins, cinnamon, and chopped nuts.
1/4 cup Fresh Parsley, chopped (or 1 tablespoon dried)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the pastry: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, salt, and baking powder. Add the margarine and mix until the dough forms rice-sized bits.
2. Gradually add cold water, stirring, then add more water as needed until the dough forms a ball. Turn the dough onto wax paper and press into a ball. Refrigerate until the filling is prepared.
3. Prepare the filling: If using fresh fruit mixture, chop the fruit and set aside in a bowl. For the meat filling, heat oil in a frying pan. Add chopped onion and garlic, sautéing on medium heat for 3-4 minutes, stirring frequently.
4. Add crumbled ground beef to the pan and cook until browned. Stir in all other ingredients except water. If needed, add up to 2 tablespoons of water. Cook for 3 minutes, then remove from heat and set aside.
5. Form the empanadas: Lightly grease a cookie sheet. Work with small portions of dough at a time. On a floured surface, roll out the dough to about 1/8 inch thickness. Cut rounds measuring approximately 4 inches in diameter.
6. Place a teaspoon of filling in the center of each round. Fold half the dough over to cover the filling, forming a half-moon shape. Press a fork dipped in flour around the edges to seal. Brush the top with beaten egg for glaze.
7. Bake empanadas at 400°F (200°C) for 15 to 20 minutes, or until golden brown. Serve warm. For preparation ahead, cool on wire racks, then wrap airtight and freeze. Makes 24 small empanadas.
